bullets.text: Annual CR 92.6; net income just under $1.2B; profitability exceeded 96 target but margins compressing toward target levels; expense ratio 21.1 (vs. 20.2 prior year) as falling average premium deleveraged expense gains.

Written premiums fell 3% YOY — rate decreases not fully offset by policy growth; policies in force reached all-time highs in Personal Auto, Special Lines, and Commercial Auto; Direct auto PIF +7%, Special Lines +8%, Commercial Auto +7%.

~$3B returned to shareholders in 2007 via $2/share extraordinary dividend plus repurchases; $1B hybrid debt issued; Gainshare factor 0.74 yielding $0.145/share variable dividend; portfolio total return 4.7%; debt-to-capital 30.6%.

Mid-2006 rate reduction strategy largely completed by Q4 — future rate actions to be determined by market conditions; bodily injury severity trend increasing faster than limits profile explains; reserve increase $4.9M in Q3 ending sustained favorable development streak.

Agency auto PIF -1% reflecting competitive pricing sophistication improvements among major competitors; competition now presenting rates alongside Progressive more frequently; Direct growing 7% as consumers increasingly prefer Internet channel.

Consolidated to single Progressive brand; reorganized Agency and Direct into one Personal Lines organization to reduce redundancy; Immediate Response Vehicle re-established as brand icon; Progressive Field naming rights with Cleveland Indians announced January 2008.

First customers using wireless vehicle-data UBI technology — new hardware better matches concept than prior devices; Keynote #1 ranked auto insurance website for 11 of 12 periods since 2000; e-signature and e-fulfillment in both channels.

Personal auto CR stable 92.5-93 across all three quarters; Agency and Direct earned premium per vehicle down 5% YOY; early signs of bodily injury trend increasing faster than expected; severity inflation on large losses most notable concern.

Agency and Direct merged into single Personal Lines organization in September — channel pricing maintained but product design, management, and policy servicing consolidated to eliminate redundancies and increase speed to market.

Pet Injury Coverage introduced — fresh message generating surprisingly positive consumer reaction; homeowners bundling available through Agency and Direct; usage-based wireless technology first customers live as 2008 outlook item.
